by the Bricklayers' union refusing
to furnish men until settlement
had been effected. Burns & Hall
were under contract to complete
the building in a certain time or
pay a heavy forfeit.


The cement workers will take the
entire trouble up to their interna-
tional and it is possible that the
American Federation of Labor will
eventually make a settlement OF
the differences.


Members of other unions are out
spoken in disavowing the action OF
the bricklayers and hod carriers in
this particular instance and say
that the matter should have been
held in allegiance until the ques-
tons of jurisdiction between the
interested unions had been finally
settled. They say that actions OF
this nature are uniustified and tend
to bring discredit upon the union
movement everywhere. The brick.
layers are not affiliated with the
Structural alliance or the american
Federation of Labor.
